meta.name: Ireland
meta.aboutStudying: Ireland is the only English-speaking country in the Eurozone, making it a magnet for international students. Its universities rank highly for the humanities, technology and life sciences, and the country hosts the European HQs of Google, Apple, Meta and Pfizer — offering exceptional graduate career prospects. The famously warm Irish welcome makes settling in easy.

tips.title: EU Free Fees Scheme
tips.text: EU/EEA students qualify for the Free Fees Initiative and pay only a Student Contribution of around €3,000/year. Non-EU students pay €10,000–25,000/year. Dublin is expensive (€1,200–1,800/month) so consider Cork or Galway to save significantly.

tips.title: Tech Career Hub
tips.text: Ireland hosts the European headquarters of Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft, LinkedIn and Pfizer. Graduate employment rates are among the highest in Europe, and the Third Level Graduate Scheme lets you stay and work for up to two years after finishing.

tips.title: English-Speaking & Eurozone
tips.text: Ireland is the only native English-speaking country still in the EU after Brexit. You get globally recognised English-language degrees plus full EU membership benefits. The literary heritage (Joyce, Yeats, Wilde, Beckett) and lively pub culture are legendary.

universities.name: Trinity College Dublin
universities.desc: Ireland's oldest and most prestigious university — a global top-100 institution and home to the famous Book of Kells.

universities.name: University College Dublin
universities.desc: Ireland's largest university and one of Europe's most internationally diverse, with the highly ranked Smurfit Business School.

universities.name: University College Cork
universities.desc: A research-led university on a beautiful riverside campus, known for food science, medicine and a strong student culture.

universities.name: University of Galway
universities.desc: Set in Ireland's vibrant cultural capital on the wild Atlantic coast — strong in medicine, marine science and the arts.

universities.name: Dublin City University
universities.desc: A young, enterprise-focused university with strong industry links and Ireland's leading reputation for employability.

universities.name: University of Limerick
universities.desc: A modern riverside campus university famous for its cooperative education (paid work placement) programmes.

universities.name: Maynooth University
universities.desc: Ireland's fastest-growing university, set on a historic campus just outside Dublin, with strong humanities and computing.

Dublin.desc: Ireland's vibrant capital — a compact, walkable city packed with history, literature and pubs, and the European HQ of the world's biggest tech firms.
Dublin.studentLife: Excellent — two major universities, huge international community, legendary pub culture.
Dublin.transport: LUAS trams, DART trains and buses; very walkable centre.
Dublin.nightlife: World-famous — live trad music, literary pubs and a buzzing club scene.

Cork.desc: Ireland's relaxed and characterful second city — built on islands in the River Lee, famed for its food scene, friendly locals and a much lower cost of living than Dublin.
Cork.studentLife: Vibrant — UCC gives the city a youthful, creative energy.
Cork.transport: Compact and walkable; 2.5 hrs to Dublin by train.
Cork.nightlife: Excellent — a famously sociable city with great live music.

Galway.desc: Ireland's bohemian cultural capital on the wild Atlantic coast — a colourful, music-filled student city and gateway to Connemara and the Aran Islands.
Galway.studentLife: Outstanding — a small, intensely sociable city dominated by students and artists.
Galway.transport: Very walkable; 2.5 hrs to Dublin by bus or train.
Galway.nightlife: Legendary live music — trad sessions in pubs every night.

Limerick.desc: A historic Shannon-side city undergoing a creative revival — home to the University of Limerick's renowned campus and one of Ireland's most affordable student destinations.
Limerick.studentLife: Strong and growing — UL's co-op programmes attract ambitious students.
Limerick.transport: Compact centre; 2 hrs to Dublin, 1 hr to Cork and Galway.
Limerick.nightlife: Lively student scene with a famous rugby culture.

cityCards.desc: Ireland's vibrant capital — history, literature, pubs and the European HQ of the world's biggest tech firms.
Ireland's relaxed second city — a foodie capital with a much lower cost of living than Dublin.
Ireland's bohemian cultural capital on the wild Atlantic coast — colourful, musical and student-filled.
A historic Shannon-side city — home to UL and one of Ireland's most affordable student destinations.
